title: Longest Word Buildable by Successive Prefixes
description:
Given a list of distinct lowercase words, find the longest word that can be built one character at a time by other words in the list. Every prefix of the word must also be present in the list. If multiple answers have the same length, return the lexicographically smallest. Use a trie and a DFS that only descends through nodes whose prefixes are complete words.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ |words| ≤ 10^5
- Sum of word lengths ≤ 2·10^6
- Words contain 'a'..'z' only
explanation:
Intuition
The constraint that every prefix must exist means valid paths descend only through nodes marked as end. A DFS that filters by end nodes constructs candidates and tracks the best by length then lexicographic order.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Insert words into a trie marking end flags
2. Main Algorithm: Depth-first search from the root, continuing only through end nodes
3. Return Result: Update the best answer by length then lexicographic order

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(T) where T is total characters
- Space Complexity: O(T) for the trie

Key Insights
- Filtering by end nodes prunes invalid branches early
- Sorting child keys provides deterministic lexicographic order

Common Pitfalls
- Allowing non-end prefixes yields invalid candidates
- Not tie-breaking lexicographically may return wrong answer

testCases.explanation: Two length-5 words, pick lexicographically smaller

hints: Only descend through nodes marked as complete words
Track best by length then lexicographic order
Sort child keys for deterministic traversal
